enginesAndModelsDesc: Manage transcription engine runtimes, speech models and GPU acceleration
translationServicesDesc: Configure translation providers, API keys and connectivity tests

modelDesc.tiny: Fast text output, average transcription quality.
modelDesc.base: Fast text output, average transcription quality.
modelDesc.small: Moderate text output speed, good transcription quality.
modelDesc.medium: Slow text output, best transcription quality.
modelDesc.large: Slow text output, best transcription quality.
modelDesc.largeTurbo: With little loss of accuracy, the transfer speed is greatly improved
modelDesc.tinyEn: English only, fast text output, average transcription quality.
modelDesc.baseEn: English only, fast text output, average transcription quality.
modelDesc.smallEn: English only, moderate text output speed, good transcription quality.
modelDesc.mediumEn: English only, slow text output, best transcription quality.

deleteModelDesc: After deletion, if you need to use the model again, you need to re-download.
pathConfigDesc: If you're unsure about the configuration, keep the default settings.
pathConfigDemo:
Filename configuration supports ${fileName} ${sourceLanguage}, ${targetLanguage}, ${model}, ${translateProvider} as variables

${fileName} represents the file name of the current processed file

${sourceLanguage} represents the source language of the video configured

${targetLanguage} represents the target language of the translation configured

${model} represents the whisper model configured

${translateProvider} represents the configured translation service name

For example, if a video file is named demo.mp4, the source language is English, the translation language is Chinese, and the path configuration is ${fileName}.${targetLanguage}

Then the generated subtitle file name is: demo.zh.srt
logsDesc: View application logs and system messages for debugging
updateReadyDesc: New version {version} has been downloaded and is ready to install.
downloadingUpdateDesc: Downloading version {version}. You will be prompted to install when complete.
gpuAccelerationEnabledTip: GPU acceleration is enabled. Click to view settings.
gpuFallbackToast: GPU acceleration unavailable, automatically switched to {{backend}}
gpuMigrationNotice: This version adds universal GPU acceleration (Vulkan), enabled by default. Adjust in Settings → GPU Acceleration.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.macDamagedQ: macOS says the app is damaged and can't be opened?
faq.macDamagedA: This is macOS quarantine for unsigned apps, not actual damage. Run the following command in Terminal, then reopen the app:
faq.cudaCrashQ: Transcription crashes after enabling GPU acceleration?
faq.cudaCrashA: Usually the acceleration package version doesn't match your GPU driver. Go to Resources → Acceleration and switch to another package version, or fall back to CPU mode — slower, but it always completes.
faq.slowDownloadQ: Model downloads are slow or keep failing?
faq.slowDownloadA: Go to Resources → Models and switch the download source, then retry. You can also download the model file manually and add it via Import Model.
faq.translateFailedA: First verify the provider works via Test Translation in Resources → Translation (key, network, quota). Then back in the proofread editor, use the Failed only filter with Retranslate failed to fix them in one go.
faq.subtitleGarbledQ: Burned-in subtitles show garbled text or boxes?
faq.subtitleGarbledA: The selected font likely lacks CJK glyphs. Pick a font that supports your language (e.g. PingFang, Microsoft YaHei, Source Han Sans) in the Burn to Video style settings and merge again.

downloadPill.aria: Model download progress, click to view

taskRunningPill.aria: A task is running, click to view recent tasks
taskRunningPill.label: Task running

downloadBusy: Another download is already in progress
downloadProxyHint: The download source may be blocked by a proxy/VPN. Try switching the download source, set your VPN to rule/direct mode for the download domains, or temporarily disable the global proxy while downloading.

onboarding.flowProviderDesc: Translates text into your target language (only needed for translation)
onboarding.gpuNote: GPU acceleration: an optional boost that makes transcription several times faster — you can enable it in the last step
onboarding.step2Desc: The app can't transcribe without a model. Downloads run in the background — feel free to continue
onboarding.quickChoiceDesc: Smallest and fastest, modest quality — you can switch anytime
onboarding.modelReady: You already have a model — this step is done
onboarding.downloadStarted: Downloading in the background — you can continue
onboarding.fasterWhisperModelDesc: Your current engine is faster-whisper — download its model in the Resource Hub
onboarding.funasrModelDesc: Your current engine is FunASR — download its speech models (SenseVoice / Paraformer and VAD) in the Resource Hub
onboarding.providerDesc: Only needed for translated subtitles. Free options to start: DeepLX, local Ollama, or Volcengine
onboarding.accelDescMac: Apple silicon detected — CoreML / Metal acceleration is available
onboarding.accelDescAvailable: Your device supports GPU acceleration — several times faster transcription once enabled
onboarding.step4Desc: Run the bundled 10-second sample audio through the full audio-to-subtitle flow.
onboarding.sampleExplain: Click the button below to create a task from a bundled English sample audio and start transcribing automatically. You'll see your first subtitle in seconds; if a translation provider is configured, it will be translated too.
onboarding.sampleNeedsModel: Download a speech model in step 2 first
onboarding.sampleFailed: Failed to set up the sample task. Try again or import a file manually.

alreadyLatestVersion: You are on the latest version
accelBadgePendingTip: Acceleration pack loads on first transcription
accelBadgeGpuOnlyConflictTip: GPU-only mode set, but last run used CPU
cpuModeTip: Transcription runs on CPU. Click to view acceleration options.
